data.title: Trump urges Iran to take ‘second chance’ at nuclear deal after Israel strikes
data.description:
Update:  Adds comment from Trump's recent post.

U.S. President Donald Trump, in post on the Truth Social platform, urged Iran to close a nuclear deal in his first public statement following Israel’s attack on the country.

Israel launched an attack on Iran, targeting dozens of sites, including nuclear facilities. Its Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the attack would take “many days.” Iran confirmed that one of the targets, the Natanz nuclear facility, sustained damage.

Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi called the attack a “declaration of war” in a letter to the UN Security Council, calling for immediate action from the body.

Trump, meanwhile, claimed that Israel has “a lot” of US military equipment and “they know how to use it.”

“It will only get worse! There has already been great death and destruction, but there is still time to make this slaughter, with the next already planned attacks being even more brutal, come to an end. Iran must make a deal, before there is nothing left,” Trump said in his post.

In a separate post, Trump stated, "Two months ago I gave Iran a 60 day ultimatum to ‘make a deal.’ They should have done it! Today is day 61. I told them what to do, but they just couldn’t get there. Now they have, perhaps, a second chance!"

Iran’s Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh has said previously, “If [talks] don’t reach a resolution] and conflict is imposed on use, the other side will undoubtedly suffer greater losses … We will target all U.S. bases in host countries without hesitation.”

Oil prices surged as much as 13% on Friday before trimming gains after Israel launched a series of strikes on Iran, stoking concerns of a broader conflict in a region responsible for about a third of global crude output.

WTI (CL1:COM) was up 9.15% at $74.27 a barrel at the time of writing.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

U.S. market-tied ETFs: (SP500), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU)

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Walmart and Amazon explore issuing stablecoins - report
data.description:
Some of the U.S.'s largest retailers are exploring uses for stablecoins and possibly issuing their own tokens, a move that could transform their payment systems, reducing their reliance on banks and lowering fees, according to a media report on Friday.

Walmart (NYSE:WMT), Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN), and Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE) have explored issuing their own stablecoins in the U.S., the Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter. Stablecoins are digital tokens designed to be pegged one-for-one to an actual asset, such as a fiat currency, most often the U.S. dollar.

New payment systems created by massive retailers would send shocks through the country's banks, which dominate the U.S.'s payment plumbing.

The companies' decisions partly hinge on Congress passing what's called the Genius Act, which would provide a regulatory framework for stablecoins. The bill cleared a procedural hurdle in the Senate on Wednesday, but still needs to be voted on in both chambers of Congress. 

Stablecoin-based systems could allow retailers to bypass traditional payment rails, including credit and debit cards, that charge interchange fees each time a customer makes a purchase on a card. Those systems are dominated by Visa (V) and Mastercard (MA). The banks that issue the cards, though, get the lion's share of card fees.

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is still in the early stages of its stablecoin exploration, one person told  the WSJ. Some of the discussion has focused on issuing its own stablecoin for online transactions. 

U.S. banks and other payment companies haven't ignored the potential of stablecoins. Some of the U.S.'s largest banks are in talks to issue a joint stablecoin, including JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), and Wells Fargo (WFC). JPMorgan has had its own stablecoin for years, which it uses internally, and PayPal Holdings (PYPL) has launched its own token.

data.title: Turnium Technology Group announces upsized convertible debenture offering
data.description:
• Turnium Technology Group (TSXV:TTGI:CA) announced on Friday that the company has increased its previously announced non-brokered private placement of unsecured convertible debentures for aggregate gross proceeds of up to C$2.3 million from previously up to C$2 million.
  • The company expects to close the second tranche of the Debenture Offering on or before June 26, 2025.
  • The debentures will be convertible into units of the company at a conversion price of C$0.08 per debenture unit for the first 12 months from the closing date of the second tranche of the Debenture Offering and a conversion price of C$0.10 per debenture unit for the remaining term until the maturity date. The Debentures will mature on the date that is 36 months following the closing date.
  • The debentures issued during the first tranche of the debenture offering will mature on May 27, 2028.
  • Each Debenture Unit will consist of one common share and one common share purchase warrant.
  • The debentures will bear interest at a rate of 15% per annum from the closing date, payable semi-annually in arrears in cash on June 30 and December 31 of each year.
  • The first interest payment will be on December 31, 2025, for the period from the Closing Date to December 31, 2025. A minimum of four months' interest will accrue, regardless of the date of repayment or conversion of the Debentures.
  • The net proceeds from the debenture offering, the company said, will be allocated towards expenses related to general operations including research and development of new products, sales and marketing, public company related expenses including audit and legal work, and other general operations related activities.
  • The second tranche of the debenture offering is anticipated to be completed concurrently with the second tranche of the company's non-brokered private placement of up to 28.56 million units of the company for gross proceeds of up to C$2 million.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Phillips Edison & Company announces pricing of offering of $350M aggregate principal amount of 5.250% senior unsecured notes due 2032
data.description:
• Phillips Edison & Company (NASDAQ:PECO) on Friday announced that its operating partnership, Phillips Edison Grocery Center Operating Partnership I, has priced a public offering of $350 million aggregate principal amount of 5.250% senior unsecured notes due 2032.
  • The notes were priced at 99.832% of the principal amount and will mature on August 15, 2032.
  • The offering is expected to settle on June 17, 2025.
  • The notes will be fully and unconditionally guaranteed by PECO.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: reAlpha Tech expands in Texas with launch of brokerage services
data.description:
reAlpha Tech (NASDAQ:AIRE) announced on Friday an expansion in Texas with the launch of brokerage services through its REALTOR affiliate.

"This is an exciting next step in reAlpha's national expansion," said CEO Mike Logozzo.

The real estate technology company said it plans to launch in additional states in the coming months.

data.title: Clough closed-end funds announce renewal of share repurchase programs
data.description:
The Boards of Trustees for several Clough Capital Partners L.P. closed-end funds have renewed their share repurchase programs, allowing each fund to buy back up to 5% of outstanding common shares in the open market through June 30, 2026.

 
  • Clough Global Equity Fund (NYSE:GLQ)
  • Clough Global Opportunities Fund (NYSE:GLO)
  • Clough Global Dividend & Income Fund (NYSE:GLV)

The share repurchases were originally approved in June 2023 and have been renewed annually thereafter.

data.title: Europe swings lower on rising tensions in the Middle East
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET.

London (UKX) -0.45% to 8,844.

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.48% to 23,418, Germany inflation flat at 2.1% in May. Wholesale prices in Germany increased 0.4% Y/Y in May.

France (CAC:IND) -1.17% to 7,674, France inflation drops to 0.7% in May.

Spain's annual inflation rate eases to 2% in May.

Industrial production in Hungary fell by 5.0% Y/Y in April.

The annual inflation rate in Finland stood at 0.5% in May.

Sweden’s annual inflation rate eased to 0.2% in May.

The Netherlands’ trade surplus narrowed to €9.14B in April.

Euro Area records trade surplus of €9.9B in April.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) dropped 0.85% to 545.81, on track for a fifth straight day in red, as escalating Middle East tensions turn investor sentiment. Israel’s strike targeting Iran's nuclear facilities took markets by surprise. Iran responded with drone attacks after confirming multiple fatalities.

To note, the Euro Stoxx 50 was earlier down 1.7% and the Stoxx 600 off 1.2% in Europe's premarket trade. European natural gas futures rose nearly 3%, the highest level since early April, tracking a broader surge in energy markets.

The rising tensions in the Middle-East gave a push to oil prices, while travel and leisure sector suffered. Air carriers suffered losses on the catastrophic crash involving an Air India Boeing (BA) 787-8 destined for Gatwick, UK. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis points to 2.49%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points to 4.53%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Dundee Precious Metals to buy Adriatic Metals in $1.3B deal
data.description:
Canada's Dundee Precious Metals (OTCPK:DPMLF) said Friday it agreed to acquire U.K.-based Adriatic Metals (OTCPK:ADMLF) in a cash-and-stock deal valued at $1.3B.

Toronto-listed Dundee (OTCPK:DPMLF) is offering 268 pence per Adriatic (OTCPK:ADMLF) share, consisting of 93 pence in cash and 0.1590 new Dundee shares for each Adriatic share, representing a ~50% premium to Adriatic’s closing price of 177.8 pence on May 19, the last trading day before it confirmed takeover talks with Dundee.

The acquisition would give Dundee (OTCPK:DPMLF) full ownership of Adriatic’s (OTCPK:ADMLF) Vares silver project in Bosnia and Herzegovina, which it said should help it boost production to as much as 425K gold equivalent oz by 2027.

If the deal is completed, Dundee (OTCPK:DPMLF) shareholders would own ~75% and former Adriatic (OTCPK:ADMLF) shareholders would own ~25% of Dundee's enlarged issued share capital.

data.title: Citizens Financial increases stock buyback authorization to $1.5B
data.description:
Financial institution Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) announced on Friday an increase in its share repurchase authorization to $1.5B.

Shares were -1.24% pre-market to $40.66.

The company had announced a $1.25B stock buyback program in June last year. A $300M of capacity is remaining under the prior authorization.

The repurchases may be executed in the open market or in privately negotiated transactions.
